phase-scope.phase-30: Runtime bridge implemented with fake/local adapter verification.
phase-scope.phase-34: Stabilization gate keeps runtime apply, replay, health, and revoke in the end-to-end MTProto release suite.
phase-scope.phase-37: Live edge runtime adapter added after clean-VPS Telegram Desktop proxy availability failed because no real MTProto service owned public 443.
phase-scope.phase-38: Runtime bridge must apply, revoke, health-check, and replay policy against a private DE runtime API with degraded-safe failure behavior.
phase-scope.phase-41: Runtime bridge is the production policy path again and does not depend on official MTProxy manifest sync.

V-M-044.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v

verification-levels.module-local: Runtime bridge tests use fake KPprotoN adapters and local DB sessions; they must not call live Telegram, DNS, host tools, deploy scripts, or install scripts.
verification-levels.wave-level: Runs with M-042/M-043 tests so bridge policy state cannot drift from assignment persistence or owner-safe payload generation.
verification-levels.phase-level: Phase-30 cannot pass unless bridge outage produces explicit degraded MTProto state while normal login, verified onboarding, and VPN config access remain non-blocked.

module-checks.check-1: apply-domain-policy succeeds for one issued SNI and records runtime policy state without persisting secrets.
module-checks.check-2: Startup replay loads active assignments idempotently and skips disabled or superseded assignments.
module-checks.check-3: Bridge unavailable, timeout, malformed response, or adapter exception returns degraded status without breaking KrotPN auth/VPN APIs.
module-checks.check-4: Scheduler reconciliation job is registered once and does not disturb existing subscription, cleanup, or anti-abuse jobs.
module-checks.check-5: Runtime logs and health payloads expose no tg://proxy links, derived secrets, base secret, salt, tokens, or provider credentials.
module-checks.check-6: Deploy/install surfaces remain unchanged; runtime bridge activation is configured inside backend/runtime service boundaries only.
module-checks.check-7: revoke-domain-policy removes one SNI from runtime policy state idempotently even after the assignment is marked disabled.
module-checks.check-8: Phase-34 full gate proves assignment issuance, owner access, admin revoke, runtime policy removal, and replay remain mutually consistent.
module-checks.check-9: HTTPMTProtoPolicyAdapter applies, revokes, and health-checks the local edge policy API with a token header and without exposing token or proxy secrets.
module-checks.check-10: Provisioning returns an active proxy payload only after live policy apply succeeds or explicitly reports degraded/incomplete state.
module-checks.check-11: HTTPMTProtoPolicyAdapter accepts a private DE policy API URL and rejects unsafe public/no-token configurations in static smoke.
module-checks.check-12: DE policy API timeout, auth failure, public exposure guard failure, or unreachable route returns degraded state without blocking normal KrotPN login/VPN access.
module-checks.check-13: The Erlang mtproto_proxy bootstrap URL uses POLICY_LISTEN_IP, not hardcoded 127.0.0.1, when the policy API is privately bound on DE.
module-checks.check-14: Policy apply/revoke/replay targets KPprotoN SNI policy and no official secret manifest apply is needed for active user issuance.

phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-1: Startup replay after restore loads only active assignments and skips disabled, superseded, or revoked rows.
phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-2: Runtime bridge degraded state is visible to owner/admin surfaces without blocking KrotPN login, trial, billing, or VPN APIs.

scenarios.scenario-1: New active assignment is applied to MTProto policy runtime and returns activated status.
scenarios.scenario-2: Backend or bridge restart replays active SNI assignments exactly once per reconciliation cycle.
scenarios.scenario-3: Scheduled reconciliation repairs missing runtime policy state after local adapter reports drift.
scenarios.scenario-4: Explicit admin revoke removes the runtime SNI policy while preserving the disabled assignment record.
scenarios.scenario-5: Runtime bridge failure marks MTProto proxy pending/degraded and emits safe operator evidence.
scenarios.scenario-6: Invalid SNI, disabled assignment, superseded assignment, or missing assignment is rejected before adapter apply calls.
scenarios.scenario-7: Policy apply or revoke failure does not prevent normal KrotPN login, verified-email activation, billing, or VPN config retrieval.
scenarios.scenario-8: Configured backend bridge applies an issued SNI to the live mtproto-edge policy API before the user sees an active Telegram proxy link.
scenarios.scenario-9: Configured backend bridge applies an issued SNI to the private DE runtime policy API while preserving the owner proxy payload.
scenarios.scenario-10: Adding a new Phase-41 user applies one SNI policy without restarting the DE data-plane.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Policy logs may include assignment id, user id, SNI, status, adapter name, retry count, and correlation id.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Never log or return full tg://proxy links, derived secret, base secret, salt, JWT/session tokens, email verification tokens, or raw adapter credentials.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Health endpoints and admin summaries must report aggregate bridge state, not per-user proxy credentials.

telemetry-requirements.metric-1: policy_apply_success_count and policy_apply_failure_count, tagged by safe failure code.
telemetry-requirements.metric-3: bridge_health_status with last_success_at and last_failure_code without secret-bearing fields.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-30/38 may be executed autonomously only with fake/private adapters and static smoke. Phase-37/38 live runtime rollout and public routing changes require server-side health, rollback checks, and operator Telegram proof.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: V-M-044 target tests passed locally inside the combined Phase-30 pytest run.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-14: Phase-33 runtime revoke fix added deterministic test coverage for removing an applied SNI policy.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Phase-37 added HTTP adapter coverage and live-apply provisioning regression coverage after clean-VPS Telegram Desktop reported the issued proxy as unavailable.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 verification adds private DE policy API target gates and degraded-safe failure requirements.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local execution passed private DE policy URL Settings tests, existing runtime bridge tests, and trace-marker smoke for HTTP apply/health operations.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: Follow-up verification covers POLICY_LISTEN_IP-aware bootstrap URL generation after DE mtproto_proxy failed to start against hardcoded 127.0.0.1:18080.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-18: DE runtime live check passed after rebuild: policy health OK and mtproto_proxy listener open on TCP 443 while policy API remains on the private relay address.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design restores M-044 as the production policy bridge and removes official manifest sync from the active issuance path.
evidence.evidence-9: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local tests passed for KPprotoN bridge apply/revoke/replay, scheduler replay result shape, admin runtime actions, and absence of official manifest sync in active issuance.
